join_order.build: num(uom='pure' AND !isnan(value)) self-agg GROUP BY (adsh_code, tag_code) -> MAX(value)
join_order.notes: Outer query and subquery m share identical predicates — single parallel scan over num builds thread-local max_value_maps AND fills thread-local filtered_row_buffers simultaneously. No re-read of num. After barrier: merge max_value_maps, concatenate buffers.
For each materialized row: probe sub_adsh_index with adsh_code to get sub_row O(1); check sub_fy_ok[sub_row] (direct array, O(1)); eliminates any runtime hash table build for the num->sub join.

filters.selectivity: variable — eliminates duplicate (adsh,tag) rows keeping only MAX

data_structures.max_value_map: open_addressing_hash_map(key=uint64_t packed ((uint32_t)adsh_code<<32|(uint32_t)tag_code), value=double, sentinel_key=UINT64_MAX, cap=next_pow2(79000*2)=131072, slot=16B, footprint=2MB) — thread-local instances merged after parallel scan
data_structures.sub_fy_ok: direct_array(bool[86135]) — sub_fy_ok[sub_row] = (sub_fy[sub_row]==2022); built from sub/fy.bin sequential scan; 86KB fits in L1/L2 cache
data_structures.filtered_rows_buffer: thread-local std::vector<struct{int32_t adsh_code; int32_t tag_code; double value;}> — concatenated post-barrier; ~79K total entries ~1.2MB
data_structures.result_candidates: std::vector<ResultRow{double value; int32_t name_code; int32_t tag_code;}> — populated from probe pass; partial_sort top-100

parallelism.scan_phase: parallel morsel scan over num 39.4M rows; each thread owns thread-local max_value_map (full cardinality cap=131072, C9) + filtered_row_buffer; no cross-thread coordination during scan
parallelism.merge_phase: sequential merge of <=64 thread-local max_value_maps (~79K total unique keys, fast linear pass); concatenate filtered_row_buffers into single contiguous array
parallelism.probe_phase: sequential single-threaded pass over ~79K materialized rows — small enough that parallelism overhead exceeds benefit

indexes.purpose: join_lookup — probe adsh_code -> sub_row for num->sub join; zero build cost (P11)

aggregation: hash_group_by — open-addressing map keyed on packed uint64_t (adsh_code,tag_code), storing MAX(value) as double; thread-local per-thread maps merged sequentially after scan barrier; final merged map probed for equality check in pass 2

output.string_quoting: double-quote all string columns: printf("\"%s\"", str) for name and tag (C31)
output.tiebreaker: stable three-key comparator: value DESC then name_dict[name_code] ASC then tag_dict[tag_code] ASC (C33)

correctness_rules: C2: load num/uom_dict.txt at runtime to find pure_code (int16_t); load sub/name_dict.txt and num/tag_dict.txt for output decoding — never hardcode codes
C9: max_value_map cap = next_pow2(79000*2) = 131072; thread-local maps sized for FULL key cardinality not cardinality/nthreads
C15: max_value_map key MUST pack BOTH adsh_code AND tag_code — missing either dimension causes wrong MAX grouping
C20: initialize max_value_map sentinel key with std::fill(slots, slots+cap, SENTINEL) not memset — UINT64_MAX sentinel for key
C24: all hash table probe loops use bounded for-loop: for(uint32_t p=0; p<cap; ++p) — no unbounded while
C27/C32: sub_adsh_index header parse (sub_cap, sub_mask=sub_cap-1, sub_ht=raw+4) declared at function scope before any loop — not inside lambda or block
C29: value column is MAX (not SUM) — double bit-equality comparison against max_value is safe since both read from same binary; output via %.10g not %.2f
C31: double-quote name and tag strings in CSV output
C33: sort comparator includes three stable keys: value DESC, then name string ASC, then tag string ASC to ensure deterministic top-100
